stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In the years following World War II, Toyo Miyatake reopened his photography studio in Los Angeles.
  • He began documenting the lives of Japanese American families resettling on the West Coast.
  • In 1944, he published a portfolio of photographs taken inside the Manzanar incarceration camp.
  • His portfolio was the only contemporaneous photographic record of camp life made by a Japanese American incarceree.
  • It is still available to researchers in archives and online.
stem: The student wants to emphasize the uniqueness of Miyatake's accomplishment. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Miyatake was the only Japanese American incarceree to make a contemporaneous photographic record of camp life.
After reopening his Los Angeles studio, Miyatake began documenting the lives of Japanese American families resettling on the West Coast.
Miyatake reopened his Los Angeles studio, which had been closed during the war, after returning from incarceration.
The portfolio Miyatake published about Manzanar is still available to researchers in archives and online.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. By indicating that Miyatake's portfolio was the only contemporaneous photographic record of camp life made by a Japanese American incarceree, this sentence emphasizes the uniqueness, or one-of-a-kind nature, of Miyatake's accomplishment.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence describes some of Miyatake's accomplishments, it does not emphasize their uniqueness. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence describes a fact about Miyatake's career, it does not emphasize that any of his accomplishments were unique. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence provides information about Miyatake's portfolio, it does not emphasize what made it unique.
